entry.title: Manufacture Part Number visibility in VSS Screens
entry.content:
Hi All,

Our organization recently underwent a SAP implementation process and currently utilizes the Vehicle Sales Service Dealer Management System (DMS). Initially, a decision was made by management to use internal SAP part numbers without the Manufacturer Part Number (MPN), resulting in most VSS screens not displaying the MPN.

Example:

 

Considering our company's primary focus on vehicle aftersales, there's a growing need among end-users to display the Manufacturer Part Number (MPN) on their screens. This is because item descriptions often lack clarity in identifying the correct part. The MPN serves as an intelligent number, facilitating easier identification. The solution offered by the Application team involves using MB51, but this requires entering each item individually. In a real working environment, this process can be time-consuming.

From the users they have requested to display in the VSS Screens in the below columns.

 

So; in this case;

Are there any alternative solutions available?

What are the potential outcomes if we proceed with this change request?

Have any other automotive companies experienced similar situations?

entry.title: Re: Question on Solution Manager 7.2 CCLM User CC_ALL_SID
entry.content: Hello , May I ask if the following is what you want? https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_Solution_Manager/dfb02462c453431eaf249aad96f004f8/d7d7fb56d7e4124ce10000000a4450e5.html You can edit objects in SM_WORKCENTER -> Custom Code Management -> Access Custom Code Library (objects) Best regards, Leo SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Can SAP CCO read the bar code which might contain the...
entry.content:
Same question here. I am trying to read the price from the barcode but I can't find any resources on how to do that. 
As for quantity, this worked for me:

   @PluginAt(method = "addSalesItemToReceipt", pluginClass = ReceiptPosService.class, where = POSITION.BEFORE)
    public void beforeAddSalesItemToReceipt(Object caller, Object[] args, Object stack) {
    if (runok) {
   
        if (forcedQuantity.get() != null) {
            //Check the ThreadLocal which was set earlier. If a price was set, then the forth parameter is changed accordingly, since this holds the price for the sales item
            args[4] = forcedQuantity.get();
        }
    }
    }

Where 
private ThreadLocal<BigDecimal> forcedQuantity = new ThreadLocal<>();
forcedQuantity.set(new BigDecimal(quantity));

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 4
entry.content: Excellent! This is how we all learn... by reading other's code. We are so lucky nowadays... when we have places like GitHub where we can go and read tons of good quality code.

entry.title: Is it possible to add own international address version in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi there

2189610 - How to activate and create country specific International address version? - SAP for Me

As above, in classic ECC it is possible to add own international address version, and I would like to confirm whether it is available or not in S/4HANA public cloud.

I  checked the help page of international address version for S4HC below, and my understanding is that we can only activate the versions which provided as standard setting, and cannot add our own versions.

International Address Versions | SAP Help Portal

I appreciate your support.

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: [BUG] blog shows up under untagged tag
entry.content:
Hi @audreystevenson, now it has completely broken down. None of the posts have the SAP Business One tag:

Also, in case it is related, the SAP Business One tag page is very slow to open. In fact it has been for a while now.

entry.title: SAP - Connectors give Invalid URL error when trying to call the API endpoint.
entry.content:
I'm implementing connector to our external system using SAP connectors. I have set up all the required fields including the base url. I have created and resource and I'm trying to try it executing, and getting this error,

{"requestId":"66050540e4b0579815d6b4a3","providerErrorCode":400,"message":"Bad Request","providerMessage":"<HTML><HEAD>
<TITLE>Invalid URL</TITLE>
</HEAD><BODY>
<H1>Invalid URL</H1>
The requested URL "http://%5bNo%20Host%5d/rest/310/OrderDetail", is invalid.<p>
Reference #9.974ddb17.1711605056.4c8c89fa
</BODY></HTML>
"}

I can notice that "Host" in the url, is this supposed to be resolved automatically ? Or is that the problem ? I mean when decoded this URL it becomes like this,

http://[No Host]/rest/310/OrderDetail?

I do not understand how this "Host" is resolved to my base url. Can someone please help ?

Note : I have some pre-request hooks to modify the headers as once of the server requirement.

 

 

Thanks.Dilan

entry.title: Reading an attribute value one Dim1 member, where the Dim1 member is an attribute of another dim2
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

In SAC model, we have Material dimension, and it has a text attribute: FreightCode,

We have another dimension called FreightCode, and it has a numeric attribute called: FreightCost

In the Data Action, for a material, how can we get the FreightCost for the matching FeightCode. 

This is static (master data) hence we would like to work through dimensions. 

There are 1000s of materials, and only 20 Freight Codes, so that when a FreightCost changes, it applies to all the materials that share the same FreightCode.

Thanks,

SP9

entry.title: Re: Receiver XI adapter in SAP CPI
entry.content: Hi This flow is from CPI to SAP S4 HANA system, so no where used OAuth2 authentication

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to add own international address versi...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

your understanding is correct - in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition you cannot create your own customer specific address version.

Best regards,

Nils Buerckel

entry.title: How to set the overall status of a Value Contract to "In Process" after creating a Subsequent Order?
entry.content:
The overall status of a value contract remains as "Open" , how can this be set to "In Process".


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to set the overall status of a Value Contract to...
entry.content:
• There are only two statue values that a Value Contract can be in, these are Open and Completed.
  • For a value contract to be set as completed, the user must set a reason for rejection.
  • To set the rejection reason:

    1. Open the Manage Sales Contract App.
    2. Open the relevant Value Contract.
    3. Click the change button.
    4. Select the line item of the contract.
    5. Press the Display items details button, which , on the screen, is located above the words "All Items".
    6. Once the item screen opens navigate to the Status tab.
    7. In the processing status, navigate to the Overall Status dropdown field.
    8. Select a reason in the Overall status.
    9. Click Save.
    10. Complete this for the other items if necessary.
    11. The overall status for the contract will now change from Open to Completed.

entry.title: 【Question】About using service requests of SAP for Me for public cloud environments
entry.content:
Hi everyone.

I'm using the public cloud edition.

When should I use SAP for Me service requests?

When using Private Edition, I used service requests for client copy and deletion.

Is there a case to use a service request in the public edition?

If so, please tell me when to use it.

Regards.

entry.content:
Dear Guys ,

How to access data in prod from QA Test system?

entry.title: Re: Delta extraction of ADSO from SAP BW bridge into SAP Datasphere via Transformation Flow
entry.content:
Hello @wilson_kurian3 

Wow, really helpful Blog. So far I have not yet worked with an ABAP Bridge, but somehow my feeling while reading was: "Oh! I assumed so far that the BW Bridge is similar to BW4 and that extracting data from a Changelog - even if into SAP Datasphere - would be the same like within BW?!

Did you also test the behaviour of a key figure?

If your DSO contains a value 100, and you update it to 50. I assume the Extraction table will not contain two records, -100 and +50, it will only contain one record with 50. Right?

BR, Martin

entry.title: Account member formula isn't working
entry.content:
Hello! I have an issue regarding the account dimension and formulas within it. I have an account member that is meant to lookup a value from another account member. When I created the formula it worked in the preview but no matter what filters I set in the story the value of the account member unbooked and nothing appears. I changed the structure priority to the account dimension as well. I then changed it so that instead of using lookup I utilised RESTRICT() but it still did not work. I then found out that the IF statement i was using was preventing my script from functioning correctly But I need it in order to classify a dimension value. 

As you can see the Account dimension i am trying to utilise is called Service Charges Per person(SC_PER_PERSON) and copy the values from service charges into the SC_PER_PERSON account member. But in the table only service charges show and SC_PER_PERSON will not appear as the table is set to booked only and there are no values in the member(I checked using unbooked)

Any help at all would Be greatly appreciated!

entry.content:
How to access data in prod from QA Test system?

#Sap , # QA , # Prod , # Access

entry.title: Different Integration Option based on Company Code(Ariba B&I)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are going to implement Ariba B&I for ABC and XYZ company.
Company ABC has SAP, and we are planning to integrate data via Managed Gateway.
For the other company XYZ, their backend is not SAP. So, we cannot integrate via Managed Gateway.

In this situation, we want to stop integrating XYZ data to ABC company's SAP.
Is there any way to achieve this requirement without middleware?

Thank you for your inputs.

entry.title: How can I find out which device (laptop, smartphone,...) C4C user is using when he logs in to C4C?
entry.content:
Hello SAP support,

when I am using the UI Debugger ?debugMode=true& in the C4C URL but I can't see with which device I am logged in.

Where can I see which device (laptop, smartphone, tablet,...) the user is using when he logs in to C4C? Where is it logged  in C4C which user is using which device? We need to know which device the user is using so that we can switch between different C4C mashups based on laptop, smartphone, tablet,... via a validation rule.

Thanks for your help.

entry.title: Problem using Business Object Interface in Action RAP
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am experiencing challenges with the Business Object Interface (BOI) while trying to update Sale Order information using EML. The code is processed within an action (BDEF). I am encountering two specific issues:

  1. After the BOI displays a warning ("Document is incomplete"), my action gets triggered again, resulting in it being triggered twice. Why does this happen, and how can I prevent the action from being triggered a second time after the warning is displayed?

  2. I am unable to use the reported parameter of the action; it appears simultaneously with the BOI warning. Is there a way to effectively use the reported parameter without it being affected by the BOI warning?

Image action BDEF handle:

Image not expect in UI

Any insights or advice on how to address these issues would be greatly appreciated. I'm looking for guidance on how to ensure smooth processing of the Sale Order updates and effective utilization of the reported parameter in the context of BOI warnings.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Understanding Consolidation of Investment (COI) in SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting – Par
entry.content:
I guess there might be a kind of a missunderstanding about my first proposal. The maintenance of group structure should look like this concerning the divestitured cons.units:

 first timedivestiture
legal cons.group001.2000012.2023
additional cons.group (for addditonal reporting of divestitured comp.)001.2024999.9999

Would be interested whether your approach will work, because doing ABC, but not running CoI sounds a bit confusing.

BR

Thanks,

Praveen C

entry.title: 360 Evaluation - Competency Library
entry.content:
Dear All,

Our client has a competency model consisting of four macro areas, each of which contains a number of competencies. Each competency, in turn, is assessed through questions verifying its attainment.

Within a 360-degree feedback form, the client would like a route map that allows - as first step - the employee to navigate a kind of "objective library", divided into macro areas, from which to choose only one competency. Upon selecting a competency from this library, the employee should be evaluated solely on that competency by multiple categories of raters, who should provide ratings for the questions associated with each competency.

Regarding the evaluation of the sub-questions associated with each competency, I believe we can work with a competency/behaviors logic and maybe inserting a comment for each behavior, in order to add information in a text form. However, in order to maintain a sort of competency library with these levels (Macro area - Competencies - Associated Behaviors) and allow the employee to choose the competency, how can we proceed? Do you have any workarounds to suggest?

Thanks a lot,
Federica

entry.title: Re: Default company code no longer set automatically in s...
entry.content: It has been resolved. Thank you.

entry.title: SAP BTP Innobytes – March 2024
entry.content:
SAP Business Technology Platform brings together application development, automation, data and analytics, integration, and AI capabilities into one unified environment optimized for SAP applications. Learn more: sap.com/btp

We are closing out the end of the first quarter with another round of exciting SAP Business Technology Platform innovations! This month's SAP BTP Innobytes features the highly anticipated, vector engine in SAP HANA Cloud, a wealth of new top enablement content with the Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance, and the fresh wizard layout for workspaces in SAP Build Workzone. Watch the video and explore the details below!

Data & Analytics Innovations on SAP BTP

Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance

Many of you have been looking for an easy-to-follow visualization of Master Data Management process flows. We are very excited to introduce our new Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition running on the SAP Business Technology Platform.

The various diagram types provide insights into the solution components, value, data, and process flows. They provide you with a sound understanding of the different use cases of our solution. The diagrams can help you to find out, for example, how to create or change a business partner in SAP MDG, cloud edition. In addition, you can discover the different integration options between components.

To learn more, check out:

  • Master Data Management in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub 
  • SAP Signavio Process Navigator
  • Explore the reference solution architecture for SAP Master Data Governance SAP Community blog post
  • Webinar including Demo on YouTube

Topic Expert: @C_Lindner  

New Vector Engine in SAP HANA Cloud

Long time awaited and now finally live: we are happy to introduce you to the vector engine within SAP HANA Cloud. Giving you the chance to combine your relational business data not only with graph, geospatial and JSON document data, but now also with vector data, all on a singular platform.
With the vector engine included into our multi-model database platform, you can store, process, and query vector embeddings seamlessly, especially via the powerful similarity search. You are able to retrieve contextual information for GenAI use cases and intelligent data applications as vector embeddings and benefit from the combination with native machine learning, text mining, and other advanced functions; all integrated into one platform, in a trusted and reliable manner.

To learn more about the new vector engine, check out:

  • Join the Better Business Outcomes with SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine webinar on April 4
  • Vectorize our data SAP Community blog post
  • From developer’s desk: SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine SAP Community blog post
  • Early Adopter Care Program for SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine
  • SAP HANA Cloud vector engine Demo Video

Topic Expert: @thomashammer 

Application Development Innovations on SAP BTP

New Wizard Layout for Workspaces in SAP Build Work Zone

You have been looking for predefined processes to help complete complex business flows. We are very excited to announce the new wizard Layout for Workspaces in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition running on SAP Business Technology Platform. This feature is now available for productive use.

Workspace administrators can create wizard-like predefined processes, to help users complete complex business flows. These wizards can include multiple steps and several stages. It also includes the possibility to add widgets and UI cards to each step. This ensures that business users are seamlessly guided through a wizard-like predefined process. For example, during the employee onboarding process, the new employee can be welcomed onboard with a smooth step-by-step process supported by this feature.

To learn more, check out:

  • What's New in SAP Build Work Zone SAP Community blog post
  • SAP Build Work Zone SAP Community topic page 

Topic Expert: @Yumnahabbasi

Share Your Thoughts

We would love to hear what you think about the series. How do you like the video? Are there any SAP BTP topics that you’d like us to share more about? Leave your comments down below and we’re happy to connect with you.

Thank you for your support and happy viewing, sharing, and enjoying!

If you like SAP BTP Innobytes, you may also be interested in:

  • SAP BTP Ask Me Anything: monthly LIVE Q&A series with hot topics and product experts
  • SAP BTP Garage: monthly LIVE webinar with hands-on use case walkthroughs and demos
  • SAP BTP Talk Podcast: monthly podcast covering all things SAP BTP
  • What's New with SAP BTP: quarterly LIVE series for SAP BTP innovation highlights, with demos and time for Q&A

entry.title: Re: actual and target process comparison and optimization
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for reaching out.

In Process manager, you can use The SAP Signavio diagram comparison tool to compare two diagrams. You  can also compare the revisions of the same diagram

In Process Intelligence You can visually compare process paths by placing two Process Discovery widgets next to each other.

Let me know if that's help.

Best,

entry.title: Can not open FPM link from time to time
entry.content:
Hi experts.

I have three scenarios :

1. Customer can not open the FPM link but I can.
2. Customer and I can not open the FPM link in Edge but can open it in Firefox.
3. Customer can not open the FPM link yesterday but today can open the same link.

And I debugged the backend code, even in the same condition and link. From Edge can not fetch data but Firefox can. It happends randomly. It used LIST ATS IDA.

         SELECT COUNT( * )

              INTO lv_records_count
              FROM zc_xpld_res
              WHERE (<fs_whr_str>-where_tab).

Does anyone have experience with it?

entry.title: Getting an error when opening Fiori apps in a custom role.
entry.content:
Hello!

We have created a custom business role including the below 2 apps and we are faced with an error "Set only one date range for key date restriction in the user role." when we try to click "GO" in the apps.

F0711 Manage Customer Line Items

F0712 Manage Supplier Line Items

I haven't touched any value related to key date restriction while creating the role so I have no idea how this error is caused.

I would appreciate it if there is any solution for this error.

Thank you in advance for your support!

Fujita

entry.title: 360 Evaluation of Competency to PM Form
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have opened another post in order to describe a need about our client, who wants to implement 360 Reviews. Please refer to this post in order to understand another aspect of their entire requirement, if you want: https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-q-a/360-evaluation-competency-library/qaq-p/13652123#M34370

Please, take into account that the client wants to implement the 360 feedback not for developmental purpose but as a distinct step in the evaluation process. This is the reason why, thinking on the possibility to have a competency in the traditional PM form, the client would like the assessment of a competency from the 360 form to be reflected in the same competency within a traditional Performance Management form (non-360), where the competency is listed.

For instance, envisioning that in the traditional Performance Management form, the competency is labeled as 'Problem-Solving,' the same competency must be evaluated in a 360-degree manner by selecting specific raters, whose average rating should then feed into the 'Problem-Solving' competency within the classic PM form.

I am aware that currently there is no system-level communication between 360 and Performance Management forms. So I was evaluating The Ask for Feedback/Get Feedback feature as a possible suitable functionality to use only to the classic form to meet this requirement, bypassing the use of the 360. Alternatively, are there any additional workarounds that could achieve this? Do you have suggestions or point of attention?

We suggested to the client to keep the two evaluations separate and to allow the managers to view the results of the 360 form, so they could then manually incorporate it into the competency evaluation in the classic PM form. However, as it involves manual activity and potential errors, the client seeks alternatives to manage a smooth transition between the concept of 360 evaluation and non-360 evaluation.

Thanks a lot,
Federica

entry.title: Details for C4C SSO configuration
entry.content:

Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Details for C4C SSO configuration

We have the help doc Configure Your Solution for Single Sign-On details how to configure SSO in C4C.

If more information required, you may make use of the Community and SAP for Me.

Please let us know if any further queries.

entry.title: Re: Hi Team, comma separator for the values
entry.content: If you want a quick answer, please give details about "display" and about the type and length of your variable (DEC, string, etc.). Display where? If it's just to convert from string to string, don't say "display". Also indicate your ABAP version. I hope you understand, for your future questions.

entry.title: Re: How to change 'Started By' field value in SAP BPA process Instance
entry.content: This is because you used authentication by client credentials (aka as technical user) created in service key of process automation service. If you want to have name of the user there, you have to call the POST endpoint of WF API through different authentication process - eg. SSO or SAML Bearer or User token exchange or others. You can find more here or here

entry.title: Re: S_DEVELOP and S_RFC Auth Objects
entry.content: Thanks for adding the hyperlink to the note. S_RFC and S_DEVELOP are documented in the SAP Library, I don't think it's worth repeating here. To all: the list of authorizations is in the attached document "Authorization_Objects_List_Conversion_2383530.xls".

Roy

entry.content: Hi Sai, are there any News regarding this topic? We still see only the same information on the roadmap without more details, Cheers Philip

entry.title: How to distribute to multiple agreement, when using multiple partner functions at CPC item level
entry.content:
Hello, 

Requirement is to maintain the different Partner functions like GS in different line items of CPC and distributed to different scheduling agreement. 

Is it possible to distribute the CPC to different SA, if we maintain different Goods Supplier as a Partner function at the CPC item level. 

Thanks,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to distribute to multiple agreement, when using m...
entry.content:
Hello,

For each central contract item, whether one or several distributed outline agreements are created in their respective connected systems, depends on the information that you entered, such as the outline agreement category, outline agreement type, purchasing organization, purchasing group, purchasing organization, company code, payment terms, and document currency. This information serves as the split criteria for the distribution of the central contract item. Partners are not considered as part of the split criteria, so it is not possible to create different scheduling agreement, if the items only differ based on partners and not based on the split criteria mentioned before. (See SAP Help)

To create different agreements in connected system you either need to have at least one different split criterion or it is necessary to create multiple Central Contract.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: CDS for Manage Customer Line Items
entry.content: Wanted to understand the Fieldname or CDS to get OPEN ITEM Managed Items(Accounting Document number wise).

entry.title: SAP WORKFLOW
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

when a person is on absence to approve purchase orders. he will be putting substitute for another person to approve it.

 

The issue is substitute person has lower value limit to approve it however he is able to do approvals without any issues.

Is there a way where we can maintain the approval process substitue only to same level of value limit or higher value limit instead of lower value limit.

Is there any settings that are available to do this in workflow...?

entry.title: Re: Guided Tours tab not visible to learners
entry.content:
Hi @ShaneLipke 

Now, the guided tour created on Fiori Home page shows up for users after publishing. However, guided tour under specific Fiori tiles, still don't show up.

Any suggestions please.

entry.title: NFS for RISE with SAP Private cloud
entry.content:
My customer is migrating from on premise to RISE with SAP private Cloud Edition Premium option. They are using a NFS for storing files this is mounted to AL11 on premise.

What is equivalent option in RISE with SAP private Cloud Edition Premium option

Thank you

Amar

entry.title: Re: Good to know: Firefighter Session related changes from Access Control Service Pack 21
entry.content:
Dear Rporanki,

background jobs are one background processing which is not supported, however I don't speak about jobs itself. Just purely using Trusted RFC has no such known side effects. Trusted RFC is allow you to logon to a backend system without a password. If you are using applications which initialize Background RFC (bgRFC) then you can face an issue what you describe, however bgRFC is background processing which is not supported with Firefighter. 

I suggest to check the newly released enhancement (Note 3435355/3435356) which allows you to disable the locking mechanism, however using background processing might produce logs outside of the Firefighter Session which can be a compliance issue. SAP can not take any responsibility for using the Firefighter functionality for this purpose.

Best regards, 
Mate

entry.title: Re: Hi from Southern California
entry.content:
Hello @BrianVanderwiel welcome to SAP Community. I am Svea, part of the SAP Community team, and I would like to give you some recommendations how to start here.

First of all, the "Tour the SAP Community"-tutorial is the best starting point. It helps you to understand the features a bit better.

Then there are:

  • topic pages: updates from SAP product teams
  • technology blog posts : experience from other community members
  • technology Q&A forum: ask your question here or see, if you can answer some of them
  • groups: there is a wide range of location groups and interest groups, which you can join to interact with other community member

That's not all, but a good starting point, and I hope, this helps you. Feel free to explore many more areas in SAP Community. 

Let me know if you have questions.

entry.title: Re: Account member formula isn't working
entry.content:
Hi ahfadoo,

In the formula, you need to specify whether you want to filter by ID or Description.

Formula:

Output:

entry.title: Alternative Payee Field is inactive during FB60 Transaction
entry.content:
I have updated the permitted payee field in the vendor master record. 

But still while doing FB60 transaction, Alternative Payee field is not active.

I am using S4HANA private cloud system.

Is there any configuration which is suppressing the field.

As per my knowledge, if we enter the ALternative Payee or Permitted Payee, we should be able to select the alternative payee in FB60 transaction.

entry.title: Re: I Need to Remove duplicates in sap b1 in query genera...
entry.content:
Hi,

Try to replace INNER JOIN by LEFT JOIN

LEFT JOIN OITB T4 ON T4."ItmsGrpCod" = T3."ItmsGrpCod"
LEFT JOIN PDN1 T5 ON T5."ItemCode"= T2."ItemCode"
LEFT JOIN OPDN T6 ON T6."DocEntry" = T5."DocEntry"

Hope this helps,

Son Tran

entry.title: Re: Delta extraction of ADSO from SAP BW bridge into SAP Datasphere via Transformation Flow
entry.content: Hi Abhimanyu, if you delete the change log table of your source ADSO in the Bridge then the Transformation Flow will only pick the delta records even if you select the "Initial and Delta" option in the TF. This way you can get only the delta records from the extraction table. The full load you can use the standard "Reporting" table and apply filters to fetch whatever dataset you need.

entry.title: Collection of Public Sector Innovation Award Entries and Finalists 2024
entry.content:
Introduction of Public Sector Innovation Awards 2024 Submissions: 

Transport for NSW (Australia)

Transforming the way people buy

Transport for NSW (TfNSW) is a government organization in New South Wales, Australia, overseeing various transport agencies and divisions. It focuses on developing safe, integrated, and efficient transport systems, including road, rail, and maritime networks. Committed to delivering better transport for the future, TfNSW shapes cities and communities, with customers at the forefront of its planning, strategy, and policy initiatives.

A strategic need emerged to enhance the Procurement buying experience for Transport employees. They faced challenges with outdated systems and processes, particularly following the ERP program update in 2016. Additionally, the NSW government shifted its focus towards safer and more sustainable procurement practices. With the end of support for the SAP Supplier Relationship Management (SRM) module, there was added urgency for change.

To address this, the current shopping cart functionality will be replaced with SAP Ariba Guided Buying, integrated with SAP S/4HANA. This aims to streamline processes, ensure compliance, manage risks, achieve value for money spend, and optimize the operating model for procurement efforts. Rob Halsall, executive director of NSW procurement, states that Ariba Guided Buying means “A shift away from high pre-existing knowledge and transactional processing to guided in context rational buying.”

Ministry of Sports (Saudi Arabia)

Excel among sport leaders globally and regionally

The Ministry of Sport (MoS) in Saudi Arabia plays a pivotal role in regulating and promoting the sports sector, with a focus on improving the quality of life for citizens as outlined in the Saudi Vision 2030. Their mission is to develop a thriving athletic environment and achieve excellence in competitive sports on both regional and global levels. Within the MoS, the Clubs Support Strategy department is tasked with executing and overseeing the Clubs Support Strategy program, including digital transformation initiatives, to enhance the sports landscape and support sports clubs across Saudi Arabia.

The Ministry of Sport (MoS) in Saudi Arabia aimed to modernize its clubs' board of directors elections, transitioning from manual processes to a digital platform for increased compliance and transparency. They introduced a Sports Clubs Membership & Election Platform to manage memberships and conduct elections digitally, ensuring governance and visibility across all clubs. This centralized portal, powered by the SAP Business Technology Platform, streamlined processes such as scheduling, candidacy requests, eligibility review, voting monitoring, and result tracking. Coupled with cloud-based analytics, the platform provided deeper insights and improved management capabilities, leading to higher process visibility and minimal disruptions.

Swiss Post (Switzerland)

drive change and shape the future of data analysis within HR

Swiss Post, headquartered in Bern, Switzerland, operates as a vital public service provider with a workforce of around 34,000 employees. Founded in 1849, Swiss Post's mission is to facilitate a modern Switzerland by seamlessly transporting goods, information, assets, and people through reliable services in both physical and digital realms. Committed to sustainability, Swiss Post aims to achieve net zero CO2 emissions by 2040 while upholding a strong universal service without external funding. Emphasizing economic success, ecological responsibility, and adherence to ethical standards and corporate responsibility, Swiss Post is dedicated to social responsibility.

The HR department faced challenges with data analysis due to distributed data across multiple systems and increased demand for self-service reporting. Implementing SAP Datasphere provided a solution by connecting and consolidating data sources, enabling comprehensive analysis within SAP Analytics Cloud. This empowered HR to achieve a harmonized view of data, ensuring governance and delivering state-of-the-art reports. With secure access and a self-service approach, HR gained control over data analysis, enhancing departmental efficiency and effectiveness.

City of Mississauga (Canada)

Successfully handling the ever-changing legislation

As the seventh-largest municipality in Canada, Mississauga attracts international talent and investments, with key features including the Toronto Pearson International Airport and headquarters of multinational corporations. This collaboration underscores CoM's commitment to fostering economic growth and innovation within the region. rgentIS has been partnering with the City of Mississauga (CoM) since 2015, leveraging its strategic location and vibrant economic landscape.

Convergent led the development of a comprehensive SAP solution for the City of Mississauga to streamline the administration of Growth Charges. This solution replaced the previous manual process, integrating essential functionalities for calculation, invoicing, and recording of charges. By automating workflows and notifications, the system ensures swift approvals, reduces bottlenecks, and enhances operational agility. Additionally, it sticks to strict compliance standards, providing robust internal controls and a accurate audit trail for transparency and accountability. Overall, the solution optimizes processes, improves efficiency, and meets strict audit requirements.

University of Cincinnati (United States)

Digital transformation journey towards ‘Next Lives Here’

The University of Cincinnati (UC) is a renowned public and urban research institution established in 1819. With a diverse student body exceeding 50,000, UC is committed to providing premier undergraduate, graduate, and professional education, along with experience-based learning and research opportunities. Guided by the strategic direction of "Next Lives Here," UC fosters innovation and academic excellence, aiming to make a meaningful impact in communities through inclusion and innovation. Moreover, UC stands as a sustainability leader, integrating environmental, social, and economic considerations into its practices, research, and education efforts.

The organization is transitioning from its current SAP ERP system to SAP S/4HANA Finance and SAP S/4HANA Public Sector solutions due to the end of support for the current system. They aim to improve user experience, enhance real-time visibility of financial data, and automate manual processes. Integration with SAP SuccessFactors and SAP Concur solutions is planned, along with leveraging advanced cash management functionality and embedded analytics for informed decision-making. Additionally, a new employee business partner model will be adopted for streamlined business process management.

FINALISTS 

Six out of the 13 Public Sector submission made among the list of finalists  as one of the most captivating and inspiring examples of innovation.

 Colsubsidio (Colombia)

Transitioning to a new technology for the improvement of lives

Colsubsidio, established in 1957, is a private nonprofit organization dedicated to enhancing the lives of families across Colombia. With a wide range of social services and development programs spanning sectors like housing, finance, health, education, and more, Colsubsidio serves over 1.5 million members nationwide. Over its 60-year history, Colsubsidio has earned a reputation as a trusted entity committed to enhancing the quality of life in Colombia.

The challenge faced by Colsubsidio involved scattered member data across multiple technologies, hindering efficient analysis and resulting in lower revenues and satisfaction. To address this, they aimed to enhance member experience through comprehensive analytics. SAP BTP for Cloud Foundry seamlessly integrated with existing infrastructure, enabling connectivity with open-source tools. SAP Analytics Cloud provided dynamic visualization, while SAP Data Intelligence Cloud and SAP HANA Cloud facilitated advanced analytics. This implementation significantly improved data utilization, empowering teams to generate reports and models efficiently. By centralizing data and streamlining analysis, Colsubsidio achieved strategic objectives and enhanced internal perception of their business intelligence unit.

German Federal Foreign Office (Germany)

Focusing on actual tasks instead of email responses

The Federal Foreign Office, with offices in Berlin and Bonn and a network of around 230 missions abroad, plays a crucial role in maintaining Germany's relations worldwide. Beyond political engagements, it fosters interaction and exchange in various fields such as business, culture, science, technology, environment, and development. This comprehensive approach reflects Germany's active involvement in global networks and its commitment to international cooperation across diverse domains.

The digitalization of citizen inquiries to the German Federal Foreign Office demanded a modern, flexible, and scalable system capable of efficiently managing inquiries worldwide, especially during times of crisis. Leveraging SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), a customized solution was developed in collaboration with SAP Consulting. This highly adaptable and available solution integrates artificial intelligence to enhance inquiry processing efficiency. Utilizing a side-by-side approach, inquiries can be processed independently, ensuring centralized omni-channel communication with clear roles and responsibilities. Templates maintain consistent messaging across the Foreign Office, while the cloud-native architecture ensures resilience during high-demand scenarios, such as crises. 

State Agency for Roads, Bridges and Waterways Hamburg (Germany)

Achieving long-time and aligning with political ones

The State Agency for Roads, Bridges, and Waterways oversees much of Hamburg's transportation infrastructure, managing about 2,500 bridges, as well as water management installations and river navigation. Responsible for infrastructure throughout its lifecycle, including flood and water protection, the agency plays a crucial role in maintaining and safeguarding Hamburg's essential structures and waterways.

Hamburg, the second-largest city in Germany and the EU's largest non-capital city, is home to over 5 million people. With approximately 2,500 bridges, it boasts more bridges than any other European city. To ensure efficient and safe operations, Hamburg's State Agency for Roads, Bridges, and Waterways (LSBG) relies on technology for quick identification of needs and solutions. Using SAP Business Technology Platform alongside SAP S/4HANA Cloud and other SAP solutions like SAP Asset Manager, LSBG enables real-time asset management. Field maintenance workers can assess infrastructure needs on-site, facilitating instant updates to maintenance systems and work orders. Through digitalization efforts, including the introduction of SAP S/4HANA, LSBG Hamburg streamlines the entire maintenance process—from recording structural damage to invoice processing—within a single, integrated system.

Essen für Alle (Switzerland)

Helping others in a professional manner 

"Essen für Alle" was founded in response to Switzerland's dual challenges of food waste and increasing financial vulnerability. During the COVID-19 pandemic, the NGO emerged to distribute surplus food from closed establishments to over 5,000 people monthly, irrespective of residency or financial status. With operations in Chur, Schwyz, and Zurich, the volunteer-driven organization continues its mission, delivering 18 tons of food essentials to over 1,600 food-insecure individuals every Saturday. As Switzerland sees an influx of refugees seeking safety and stability, the NGO's role in providing support becomes increasingly vital.

Essen für Alle, a volunteer-driven nonprofit NGO, with its focus on feeding the food insecure by optimizing its operations. Essen für Alle faced challenges such as process inefficiencies and security concerns, impacting volunteer scheduling, food distributions, and recipient management. They required a stable and user-friendly software architecture to address these issues. When an SAP employee proposed sponsoring the NGO's software foundation to SAP Switzerland's management team, it led to the development of a scalable and efficient cloud-based solution. They implemented a streamlined digital solution that integrates volunteer shift planning and recipient management systems. This approach ensures continuous volunteer coverage, effective monitoring of food donations, and smooth distribution management. By centralizing these processes, Essen für Alle enhances volunteer engagement, improves donation oversight, and fosters transparency in food distribution. Ultimately, this strategic initiative allows the organization to prioritize its mission of eradicating food insecurity in Switzerland.

United States Department of Justice (United States)

Getting the job done by creating a true reporting center of excellence

Established in 1870, the Department of Justice (DOJ) ensures the rule of law, national security, and civil rights under the leadership of the Attorney General. With numerous divisions and offices nationwide and globally, the DOJ manages a significant budget through the Justice Management Division (JMD). JMD focuses on fiscal responsibility, human resources, information management, and policy. Recognizing the importance of technology, the DOJ emphasizes its role in enabling various missions and aims to adapt to evolving technological changes efficiently.

In response to Presidential Memo M-19-16 urging centralized mission support capabilities, the DOJ's Justice Management Division (JMD) devised a strategic plan to eliminate redundant legacy systems and consolidate management functions. Collaborating with SAP NS2,, DOJ implemented JEDI-NG, a comprehensive data and analytics platform running on SAP technologies in AWS GovCLOUD. JEDI-NG replaced outdated systems, providing end-to-end financial management visibility and consolidating decades of data into a shared service platform. The deployment significantly improved accountability, IT service delivery, and user satisfaction, prompting interest from other DOJ components to adopt JEDI-NG. This success has spurred discussions on expanding JEDI-NG's capabilities department-wide.

Commonwealth of Pennsylvania (United States)

Giving the tolls needed to handle multiple different projects

The Commonwealth of Pennsylvania focuses on funding diverse services aimed at enhancing citizens' lives. These services encompass economic development, state assistance programs, and taxation. Embarking on its digital transformation, the commonwealth partnered with SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) in March 2023, securing the largest government CPEA contract in North America. The initial focus was on leveraging SAP solutions, including SAP Concur Solutions, to analyze employee expense reports and enhance management insights.

In its pursuit of government accountability and efficiency amidst fiscal challenges, the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania prioritized leveraging data to enhance decision-making. Its initial focus was on optimizing the production and distribution of real-time analysis of employee expense reports. Through close collaboration with partner TSC and SAP experts, the organization identified key data fields and developed custom extracts using SAP BTP within a tight timeframe of 100 days. This involved implementing SAP Integration Suite to automate data extraction between SAP Concur and SAP Datasphere, enhancing analytics with SAP Analytics Cloud. The result was a significant increase in monthly data consumption and positive feedback from business owners, heralding a new era of digital government strategy for Pennsylvania.

entry.title: Re: Delta extraction of ADSO from SAP BW bridge into SAP Datasphere via Transformation Flow
entry.content: Hi Martin, yes you are correct it will have the changed record only.

entry.title: SAP ME routing decision
entry.content:

Thanks!

 

BR,

Matic

entry.title: Re: Undelete a deleted PO item line10
entry.content: Excellent, It was working to solve my problem, thank you.

entry.title: Re: Debug background jobs in S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi, @sivathanushan_kokilathas 

If we're talking about Debugging Job, then it's not possible, here's a similar answer to that question. 

External APIs can be debugged, but you need to specify the technical user we selected in the Communication System for this scenario.

 

Hope this helps.

Regards,
Oleg

entry.title: When changing text interpretation to HTML Crystal reset the font color to black ONLY
entry.content: What a stupid software. When text interpretation set to HTML it does default text color to black. Any way to change it?

entry.title: Re: Entering debugging with ADT ends up always in SAPMSSYD
entry.content:
I would like to extend the previously provided answer with some additional comments. The previous statements of my colleague Ivan are correct for the cloud case. But ADT also provides development-tools and support-tools for on premise environments, down to SAP_BASIS 7.31 SP4, and there are currently no plans to change that.

In SAP cloud environments (e.g. BTP ABAP environment or S/4HANA ABAP environment):
- ADT uses HTTPS to communicate with the server
- (Embedded) SAP GUI is not available and therefore the use of /h is not possible
- Using the development model called ABAP Cloud is mandatory

In on premise environments:
- ADT uses SAP RFC to communicate with the ABAP server
- ADT-embedded-GUI is available and the use of /h in an ADT-embedded-GUI window is valid and can be used
- Using the ABAP Cloud development model is recommended but not mandatory
- Even if is not ideal to use classic development models (e.g. dynpro/screens/transactions) for the development of completely new functionality, it is still possible in on premise
- Furthermore it is important to distinguish between new development and support for existing development. Of course support for classic transactions and old code in on premise is absolutely valid and very important and ADT also provides the tools to do that kind of support. So it is completely fine to use the ADT debugger and other ADT support tools to do support for classic transactions etc. in on premise. I'm not aware of any plans to change that.

Regarding the original question of ending up in SAPMSSYD: We will double check this in the debugger dev team and see if there is any way to improve this situation.

entry.title: Re: Result recording is not updating/Overwriting the batc...
entry.content:
Hi Arjuna,

Yes system status SPCO is active. 

In QA07, business is using "To Insp. Stock at Lot Creation" option.

All the steps mentioned in the link, are already maintained.

entry.title: Re: What's your favorite tip for using SAP Build Apps?
entry.content:
While working with Build Apps initially, it was not obvious why certain flow functions have the secondary output node. The primary output is the success path. To the secondary output, we can attach an Alert or Toast and display error messages in case the previous operation fails. This is useful for debugging purposes.

In the example below, if the "Get Record" function fails for any reason, you can output the error message in an Alert dialog like so and identify why the operation failed and display an appropriate message to the end user.

1. Attach an Alert dialog to the second node output.

2. Select the Alert flow function node. In the dialog title, click on ABC and choose "Output value of another node" as the binding option

3.  Under select logic node,  choose "Get Record" and choose message for select node output.

entry.title: Re: provision from certain year
entry.content:
Hi Daniel,

you have a little mistake in your script.

This is the right Script:

IF INT(YEAR([d/Date])) >= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="YearStart",[d/Date]="2023",[d/IAA_GLACCOUNT]="522383200")
and INT(YEAR([d/Date])) < RESULTLOOKUP( [d/Measures]="YearStart",[d/Date]="2023",[d/IAA_GLACCOUNT]="522383200")
            +RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="Duration",[d/Date]="2023",[d/IAA_GLACCOUNT]="522383200")

THEN
            //calculate the amount of the contract by startAmount
            Data([d/Measures]="Changes",[d/IAA_GLACCOUNT]="522383200")= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="StartAmount",[d/Date]="2023",[d/IAA_GLACCOUNT]="522383200")
            // devided to the duration
            /RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="Duration",[d/Date]="2023",[d/IAA_GLACCOUNT]="522383200")
ENDIF

Thanks,

Bharath

entry.title: Re: SAP variant configuration and pricing - manual exchan...
entry.content:
Hello Radoslaw,

It is not possible to pass an exchange rate from external to be considered in the price calculation in CPS. We will do a feasibility study from our end regarding this option and will update you. 

Regards,

Kiruthika

entry.title: Re: Access to Prod from QA Test system
entry.content:
To do this you can use remote function calls with DESTINATION.
You define or can view existing destinations in transaction SM59-

 

CALL FUNCTION 'remotefunction'
  DESTINATION dest
  EXPORTING f1 =
  IMPORTING f2 =
  TABLES t1 =
  EXCEPTIONS...

entry.title: 1250000126 - No state found for ship-to address 'Ship To'; on the "Addresses" tab,
entry.content:
Dear all,

I am facing error while add the new Business Partner master data via DTW. 

Error : 1250000126 - No state found for ship-to address 'Ship To'; on the "Addresses" tab, define state information [CRD1.State]

I have attached below data for CRD1 template.   I have mentioned state and also create this state into SAP as well.   I am trying to add one bill to and one ship to address as per below details. 

 

ParentKeyLineNumAddressNameStreetBlockZipCodeCityCountryStateFederalTaxIDTaxCodeBuildingFloorRoomAddressTypeAddressName2AddressName3TypeOfAddressStreetNo
CardCodeLineNumAddressStreetBlockZipCodeCityCountryStateLicTradNumTaxCodeBuildingAdresTypeAddress2Address3AddrTypeStreetNo
C2210A22ABC15390019VasadINGJ  B503bo_BillTo   55
C2210A23ABC16390019VasadINGJ  B503bo_ShipTo   55

Kind regards,

Nikunj Mehta

entry.title: Understanding Network Partner Integration in SAP Business Network for Logistics
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Business Network for Logistics connects stakeholders across the logistics ecosystem for seamless digitalized collaboration and advanced insights. Logistics Network has a Business Partner persona termed as a Network Partner, which allows entities to partner with SAP’s Logistic Network, to provide their services as visibility providers or even bring their network and integrate with SAP Business Network for Logistics.

As depicted above, Shippers can leverage the network by connecting with existing Business Partners and reduce integration efforts and costs through instant access to vast, ever-growing carrier networks and/or already established B2B connectivity.

Network Partners can play multiple roles on the network. They can either be a visibility provider, connect with the carrier/shipper and provide near real-time updates/milestone events, or they can also extend the network with their ecosystem of carriers and facilitate services such as tendering, subcontracting, tracking and settlement.

In this blog we will explore how subcontracting and tracking are supported by some of the network partners on the network.

 

The Set-up

Onboarding

Network partners are onboarded to the network through an invitation process by SAP.

If an organization intends to be a part of the network, they can write to sap.dsc.network.enablement@sap.com, describing their request.

Network Partners can also be onboarded to the network proactively, through an invitation process by SAP based on a shipper's request.

Once the setup is done, network partners will get access to a portal with administration applications to support user management and integration configuration, and additional applications if the network partner supports other logistics processes such as subcontracting.

Portal Preview:

Note: Network Partners will also need to explicitly request for technical user a.k.a oauth client credentials. Once this is received, integration work can begin.

Business Partner Setup and Configure Partner Connections

This is an important step in establishing connectivity with the Network Partner over the network. There are 3 key aspects to consider:

  1. Business Partner set up in Shipper’s TM
  2. BNL set up with the carrier and Network Partner
  3. Configure Partner Connection pattern used.

Scenario 1: Network Partner offers subcontracting and other Logistics processes.

There are multiple ways in which the solution allows the Business Partner set up.

Approach 1:  Onboard all carriers to BNL / use specific carrier LBN ID along with network Partner in CPC

In this approach, the shipper onboards each carrier they do business with, from the Network Partner’s network to BNL, and establishes a connection with the Network Partner. 

 Business Partner mapping and BNL setup

 

Configure Partner Connections config

Things to consider:

  • In this approach, the Orders/Bookings will be seen in the carrier’s portal, and it will also be forwarded to the network partner.
  • Since there is a Network Partner involved for the subcontracting process, there is very little value added to onboard the carrier, unless there is a specific Business Process that requires the carrier to be on BNL, which the network Partner cannot support.
  • The carrier would be active on BNL for another shipper, so this unnecessarily creates orders in their tenant for which they are not expected to respond (Since Network Partners do it on their behalf).
  • High effort to onboard all carriers as most of them might not be expected to use BNL for this shipper.
  • This approach does not work for subcontracting via Freight Orders (Road).

Example:

Booking Consumer API - (Parties)

Booking Confirmation Provider API - (Parties)

Approach 2: Connect with the Network Partner via a dummy/test carrier.

In this approach, the shipper uses a dummy/test carrier and establishes a connection with the Network Partner. The setup is similar to approach 1, however here the LBN ID of the test carrier is mapped to carrier Business Partners in TM. In CPC, a specific connection is made as shown below.

This routes orders to the test carrier and also forwards the order/booking to the network partner.

 Business Partner mapping and BNL setup

 

Configure Partner Connections Config

 

Things to consider:

  • While this is okay for testing purposes, this does not add any value in production as orders/bookings are unnecessarily sent to the test carrier.
  • This approach does not work for subcontracting via Freight Orders (Road).

Example Scenario: Refer to approach 1.

Approach 3: No carrier tenant is involved. Direct connection with Network Partner

In this approach, the shipper maps the LBN ID of the Network Partner to the Carrier Business Partner in TM. And then, maintains the configure partner connects as shown below with Business Partner ID Type as, “Any LBN ID”. So, this config, allows the orders/bookings to be routed to Network Partner without any intermediate carrier. This enables direct routing of orders/Bookings to Network Partners without onboarding carriers to BNL.

Business Partner mapping and BNL setup

 Configure Partner Connections config

Things to consider:

  • In this approach, there is no need to onboard carriers to Business Network for Logistics since the Network Partner interacts with the shipper on their behalf.
  • TM can have multiple BPs per carrier based on business requirements and each of them would be mapped to Network Partner's LBN ID.
  • This is the only approach that is currently supported for Freight Orders subcontracting (Road) via Network Partner.

Examples:

Booking (Ocean/Air)

Booking Consumer API:

Booking Confirmation Provider API

Freight Order (Road)

Subcontracting Consumer API - (Parties)

 Subcontracting Confirmation Provider API - (Parties)

Scenario 2: Network Partner offers Tracking only.

In this case, typically, shippers would execute subcontracting and other planning processes with a carrier or freight forwarder and would work with a network partner to receive tracking data/milestone events.

The CPC application supports several ways to configure this scenario.

2 broad approaches are:

Approach 1: A typical setup in TM would be that the shipper would have a BP representing the carrier or the LSP. The carrier or the LSP would also be onboarded to BNL along with the network Partner.

Approach 2: The shipper would have a BP representing the carrier in TM. Carriers are not onboarded to BNL and the shipper receives tracking data from the Network Partner directly. 

In CPC any of these options can be used to set up tracking with the Network Partner.

These options relate to how a Network Partner wishes to track and also how a Business partner is set up in TM by the shipper. 

 

Example 1: If a shipper wants to utilize a visibility provider’s tracking services for all their shipments and assuming that all the carriers the shipper is doing business with are not onboarded to BNL, but have an alternate ID in the shipper’s TM the below pattern can be used.

Example 2:

If a shipper has more than one visibility provider and wants to utilize their tracking services for their shipments and assuming that all the carriers the shipper is doing business with are not onboarded to BNL, but they do have a specific alternate ID in the shipper’s TM, the below pattern can be used.

Here, the shipper will have to identify a specific alternate ID using which they can distinguish the orders being sent to specific visibility providers.

Depending on the supportability of the network partner, appropriate service can be configured.

Note: The payload examples shown above are only focused on sending and receiving the right business partner details. For detailed payload structure, refer the specifications for Freight Collaboration and Global Track and Trace.

 What should be the strategy with Network Partners?

This blog shows the supportability of the solution, the different variations, and how the APIs behave when a Network Partner is involved. It is important to determine the right strategy that works for a shipper. To do that, the below questions need to be answered as part of designing the solution. 

1. What scenarios are in scope for the Shipper-Carrier/LSP-Network Partner trio?

2. Are all carriers/LSPs in scope also in scope of the Network Partner? Is there a direct B2B connectivity scenario with one or more carriers / LSPs?

3. How is the Business Partner setup done across the components - SAP TM / S/4HANA, SAP Business Network for Logistics, and in the Network Partner's system?

4. What are the common identifiers across these components?

So, it is imperative to have a design that answers the above, and then a suitable approach or a combination of approaches can then be deployed. 

Other References

Configure Partner Connections help

Carrier Integration Youtube series

FAQs

entry.title: Re: Explaining SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) to a Beginner
entry.content: Awesome Blog Raja!

entry.title: Re: Hi Team, comma separator for the values
entry.content: Check this - https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/amount-field-with-thousand-separator/m-p/3816465

entry.title: Develop CAP Project with Lazyvim in Docker
entry.content:
Do you, like me, like to develop your CAP projects with VIM?
I have created a git repository that creates a Docker image with which you can run Lazyvim in a container and use the language server protocol (lsp) for CAP, Typescript, json, ... .

This allows you to get a similar programming experience as in VSCode. However, the fingers stay on the keyboard and do not always switch back and forth between keyboard and mouse.

Lazyvim

What is Lazyvim? It is the newest kid in the block of VIM variants. To be exact it's not a new implementation. It is based on Neovim, a very popular vim fork. On top of this it adds a bunch of plugins and configures them as well as Neovim itself. 
In the end you get a nice editor, no it's more an IDE, in the terminal

Docker Image

The Docker image you can create with the git repository does the following

  • It`s based on alpine linux, a very lightweight linux variant that is used in many Docker images
  • It installs some linux packages that are required by lazyvim. Among them is lazygit, my prefered git client in terminal that is leveraged by lazyvim.
  • It installs lazyvim.
    Technically it's not an installation, but cloning of a git repository that replaces the default Neovim configuration files by the lazyvim ones.
  • It alters the default lazyvim configuration a bit.
    The two things worth to mention here is
    • the mason-nvim.lua file which installs some language servers and
    • the introduction of cds.lua which start the cds-lsp.

The Result

A picture is worth a thousand words. So some screenshots follow.

When you first run your Docker container you have to be a bit patient (~30 seconds) cause it does all the installation work. At the end you see something like this (have a look at the README.md to see how I handle my containers).
At this point you can press the 'q' key to close the popup and start editing your files.

Btw. the next time you start your container you'll see this welcome screen.

 

If you are not familiar with VIM the next step would be to press the <space> key:

Here you see the most important shortcuts you can use to get your work done. The pink entries immediately start an action, the violet ones show you the next level of shortcuts.
In the next screenshot you see the result of 's' at the first level.

To start editing a file press <space><space>. A popup opens from which you can select a file or just enter some characters to reduce the amount shown files and then select one.

Let's come to the cds-lsp (cds language server protocol) plugin.
I opened a cds file and added a new property 'internalnotes' to the entity Books. When I define the type and begin typing I get suggestions (code completion). See the next two screenshots.

 

 If I have an error in my cds file this is immediately shown.

You can navigate to the type under cursor by pressing 'gd' (goto definition)

or show all references of a type by pressing 'gr' (goto references).

If you press <shift>-k you get the documentation of the artifact.

If you want to format a single file just press 'cf' and if you want to format all your cds files use the 'format-cds' script that comes with the cds-lsp package by pressing ':!format-cds<enter>'. Here you see the results of this call

The last screenshot is not from cds-lsp but shows my beloved lazygit which is also integrated into this Docker image and can be displayed by pressing '<space>gg'.

Conclusion

In this blog post I told you about Lazyvim, introduced my Docker image to easily use Lazyvim without manual configuration and showed you how you can easily edit cds files by leveraging the cds-lsp package.

For all those of you who like working in a terminal there is no longer a need to use VSCode.

There is much more you can do with Lazyvim and Docker but that's beyond the scope of this blog post.

entry.title: Re: Process Automation: Capital Expenditure Approval Proc...
entry.content:
Hi Ivan,

Thank you for your response.

If I were the one that was deploying the application, your suggestion would have been valid. However, the issue arises from a tutorial about importing a "Live Process" from SAP Build Process Automation Store (please check the link from the original post). The step from the tutorial doesn't really allow any user interaction besides clicking an "Add" button. Everything else that comes after that (the deployment of the CAP application) is automated.

Please let me know if you know any way of changing the node version of the app in this process, or of any other solution you can think of.

Thank you.

Best regards,
Atakan

entry.title: Re: Inbound Delivery: Output Missing
entry.content:
The correct Transaction to maintain condition records and check the output types for Inbound delivery (correspondin to Application E1 in NACE) is MN25 / MN26 / MN27

To clarify, Transaction VV21 / VV22 / VV23 is for Shipping documents (Corresponding to Application V2 in NACE)

entry.title: How to configure approval process for Sales Price?
entry.content:
How to configure approval process for Sales Price?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to configure approval process for Sales Price?
entry.content:
Hi there,

You can refer to the below help documentations (both in EN and CN):

Approval Workflows for Prices in Sales

销售价格的审批工作流

entry.title: Re: Create one single source of the truth about your products and packaging in the cloud with SAP EP
entry.content:
Hello Heinz

according to my understanding: EPD is a "very integrating tool".

But your topic focus on something different (trying to read between the lines).

EPD should support the full "life cycle". One piece of this is the "purchase order" (you try to buy e.g. a chemistry somethere).

My understanding would be: on level of "specification" (in EPD) you define your needs (e.g. quality of product etc.). EPD can be used then to start "queries"... e.g. you can ask potential suppliers: can you deliver the material/product according the "specification". If the answer is "yes".. i believe EPD will support to set up the "purchase info record" which then can be used in the SAP ERP / ECC purchasing process).

In a nutshell.. if you would need then material "X" the purchase process should make you the proposal: select seller X (instead of Y).

There: there is then no need to use the purchase process for "communication".. (e.g. that you accept only a material of quality "X" and not "Y").

This is (from my point of view) one big advantage in a potential SAP landscape,

There is only one "but". EPD is ONLY available as "Cloud" solution. So if you use still am SAP ECC/ERP system (on premise): you need a "connection" (which will come from SAP according to my understanding).

If i read betwee "your lines": you come from the other point of view.. you would like to start wih "purchase order" (or purchase order requsition etc.) to check if there is a material "X" or "Y" out there with the wished quality.

According to my understanding: this is not the intention of EPD. EPD starts much earlier (to later support the SAP logistic processes).

One challenge is: you need a "supplier" in the purchase process. A supplier can be as well a "one time" supplier.

But to use the EPD process will help a lot for selecting the wished "seller".

On top: you can collect "more information" (e.g. if we talk about chemicals.. the seller will give you e.g. the SDS/MSDS and other data).

So: EPD will change (my assumption) dramatically how we are using in the future SAP ERP/ECC (or S/4 HANA ERP) logistic process. You need "only" a clerver set up process

C.B.

entry.title: Re: Finding xml value based on attribute value in groovy...
entry.content:
I found the answer using chatgpt.

On the message:

   <HumanResource>
      <AssignmentId>
         <IdValue name="recordid">a0j6N000002HDkmQAG</IdValue>
         <IdValue name="externalid">8324137</IdValue>
         <IdValue name="customid">JOB182741</IdValue>
      </AssignmentId>
   </HumanResource>
 
The script:
import groovy.util.XmlSlurper
import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message

def Message processData(Message message) {
    def xml = message.getBody(String.class)
    def completeXml = new XmlSlurper().parseText(xml)
    def NTV_JOBID = completeXml.AssignmentId.IdValue.find { it.@name == 'customid' }.text()          
   
    message.setProperty("NTV_JOBID", NTV_JOBID)
    return message
}
 
Produces the exhange property NTV_JOBID with value JOB182741
 
Few things that i noticed
1) originally i did not do import of groovy.util.XmlSlurper
2) in walking down the tree you must NOT start at the root (HumanResource), but 1 down (AssignmentId)
3) the part between {} is quite different than i thought it would be. My guess was more like an x-path query as it would look when you set an exchange property directly in CPI. I'm still not sure what it. stands for, but it works.
 
Bit of a frustrating search but finding it in the end still makes me happy.
 
Kr
Robert

entry.title: CBTA Execution error for FIORI Scripts
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am facing below error while executing a FIORI Test Script in CBTA. Test Configuration is assigned with Fiori executable.

when executing the script with Chrome, window starts with credentials screen and terminates in TAF Login Start screen and credentials are not filling automatically. eventually report ending up with error.

Technical RFC, Test Profile user and Self checks everything works fine and there is no selection screen for Login schema.

CBTA version is 3.0.10.1. 

Could somebody please help.

Thanks and regards
Tobias

entry.title: BP Std. vs Custom General roles
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

The client has 2 divisions mapped to 2 sales orgs. The requirement is to restrict BPs access by the other division. For one division we used standard roles (General, FI customer & Customer) and Z roles for the other. The standard behavior is whenever general role is created for a BP, be it either standard or customer the other role gets automatically gets extended, i.e defualt both standard and customer general roles are always created.

We tried creating 2 user ids with specific roles assigned, it does not works. Default standard general roles needs to be given, else BP will not be created.

How do I restrict the general role that the user selects?

Any inputs will be greatly appreciated.

Thanks,

Upendra

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports bug suppressing a box (suppress formula)
entry.content:
Hi Don,

your old mail-address @Sap.com no longer works. Do you have a new one?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANa for Product Compliance Data Migration app...
entry.content:
Hello

based on this:

"Thank You Christoph for taking your time to answer my question.

The fact, though, is that I am talking about (SAP S/4HANA for) Product Compliance "new", and not (SAP S/4HANA for) EHS, or any of the classic solutions, as the customer is already on S/4HANA (2023), and not ECC.

As such I guess the scenarios I've posted, may be a bit less confusing when it comes to PC, but I admit they need some clarification; I'll address that as soon as I'm able.

Nevertheless, some your insights are already helpful in clearing some my doubts."

So if you would like to use "Product Compliance New": the challenge is different .

The topic is "much more complex" to explain. I try my very best to do it step by step (but i can only list the most imortant topics to consider):

  1. First you must understand the "CCI" ! (e.g. check: https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blogs-by-members/new-sap-ehs-management-for-beginners/ba-p/13561840 or https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/sap-quot-ehs-quot-product-structure-r-3-lt-gt-s-4/ba-p/13647512)
  2. You must understand: what is a "packed material" and "unpacked material" (according SAP definition (PLEASE CHECK ONLINE HELP !)
  3. You must understand: at which point in time you need a "CCI"? => if you do a set up of the new Product Compliance you can do it like: if the material is of type "RAW" you assume: it is chemistry and therefore you need a "CCI" (so any RAW material need a CCI ! (Mandatory !); but you can to it "optional" as well).
  4. At the end: as part of "purchasing", "Sales / delivery process", "Production process": if somebody would use a material which is "CCI" relevant.. process will stop (if set up is done correct) and somebody need to check some "Fiori App"... (check online help for more detail); based on "request": now somebody need to set up a CCI
  5. Now: on CCI level: you need to "assign" the material; in doing so: you define the material as "packed" or "bulk" (e.g. needed for Dangerous good perspective); on top: you start with data collection (e.g. composition, density etc. etc. etc.)
  6. NOW we have a big "BUT"... you MUST understand the new role concept !!! e.g. a material can be "purchased", "produced", sold/delivered/transported etc. Based on ths "role" concept (and many other information): the maintenance process will start. THIS IS NEW !!

But at the end: you have the "same" challenge in S/4 HANA as in R/3. Without a "clear" understanding regarding the "material" and the "CCI" you will not have a success.

But to use Product Compliance (S/4 HANA new version) you might have to wait (for details: please check the SAP Roadmap Explorer for the roadmap of this solution).

Hope this helps.

C.B.

entry.title: Re: Package Configuration Manager: Getting Started
entry.content: Hi, just wanted to check is this tool is still in use? Can anyone direct me to some recent blog posts?

entry.title: Re: UI5 Integration Cards for SAP Commissions
entry.content:
Dear,

Thx for this great explanation but could you reply to the above question?

@yogananda can we deploy these integration cards on SAP on-premise (fiori launchpad) and use them as "simple" tiles, and having the same result that we have with workzone?

Regards;

Marc

entry.title: Re: UI5 Integration Cards for SAP Commissions
entry.content:
@marcfrenay @jvanattenhoven 

Yes, if you have html, manifest.json and javascript files, you can deploy into on-prem fiori applications.

entry.title: Re: Hazardous substance register list (Gefahrstoffkataste...
entry.content:
Hello

regarding;

"Hello again.

Maybe PC's Substance Volume Tracking function can be used to develop a Fiori App to deal with your need? 
I'm just theorizing at the moment, mind you, and I know it might be a way too long stretch. "

PAY ATTENTION: SAP will clearly align "Product Compliance" and "EHS"; According to my understanding: you need to differentiate the "license" part and the "Software" part.

The new EHS will support your deman clearly. The new SVT is not "useful" at it is linked to the "old" SVT and therefore is not interested regarding the topic: what is the current stock for material X in plant/storage location).

Assumption is: with 2025 Product Compliance and EHS will come "closer" together. Please check SAP Marketplace to find official announcements regarding "Roadmap" of both solutions (and timeline for getting some functionality).

On top: check SAP Roadmap Explorer to get more insight here

With you the best

C.B.

entry.title: Re: Floorplan: Overview page, Waterfall Chart is not working
entry.content:
Found the problem .. the faul is in the oData Service .. 

Before:
My entityset includes only one line with different entities ... -> not working

After:
Entityset includes multiple lines -> working

entry.title: SAC to BigQuery data transfer using Datasphere?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have a question. Is it possible to set up a data transfer flow from SAC to BigQuery using SAP Datasphere? I'm looking for a way to perform such operation either in real-time or at scheduled intervals. If anyone has some other ideas I'd love to hear them as well.

KR,

Peter

entry.title: Re: Getting back to Standard Proposals with SU24 Authorisation Variants
entry.content:

I am looking for a possibility though to copy SU24 variants. Sometimes you need a variant that is only a bit different and now you have to recreate the same variant again with this minor difference as there seems no possibility to copy.

Also the download/upload functionality does not support this scenario.

Is there a solution or workaround here?

Thank you!

Lodewijk
